items.title: Albanese urges Australians to 'live life as normal' during fuel crisis in rare national address
items.summary: Anthony Albanese asks Australians to trade their cars for public transport if they can, while reassuring those who need to drive they can continue to do so amid the global oil crisis.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Live Nation's Love Song 2026 finds gen Z embrace retro tech, scrapbooking, in-person connection
items.summary: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

items.title: Class action alleges GrainCorp had 'expand first, fix problems later' approach to production
items.summary: A class action of 18 people from the small Victorian town of Numurkah alleges that agribusiness giant GrainCorp's increased production at the site has led to offensive noise and odour for residents.

items.title: Anthropic boss makes big call on Australian copyright as artists say pay up
items.summary: The AI company's billionaire boss says he is not trying to convince Australia to change its mind on protecting the copyright of artists.

items.title: Years after devastating floods, residents watch as former homes demolished
items.summary: Former residents of an Ipswich unit complex repeatedly damaged by floods have watched as the buildings are destroyed for good.

items.title: WA government to use emergency powers to force fuel companies to reveal supply chain information
items.summary: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

items.title: Tuesday no longer cheapest day for petrol in Perth as Middle East war disrupts fuel price cycle
items.summary: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.

items.title: Graham Arnold coaches Iraq to first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years
items.summary: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Boliva.

items.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia
items.summary: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos senior leaders decider to stand down winger Josiah Karapani
items.summary: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds says the club's senior leaders took a non-negotiable stance on culture and behaviour to stand down winger Josiah Karapani for one match.

items.title: Former University of Sydney academic appears in Iranian propaganda video
items.summary: An Australian academic has appeared in an Iranian propaganda video supporting a toll system imposed by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on foreign merchant shipping.

items.title: Daniel's Law child sex offender register website receives more than 290,000 visits in three months
items.summary: Queensland's first public child sex offender register, listing reportable offenders who have failed to comply with their reporting obligations or whose whereabouts are unknown, has been praised for protecting children.

items.title: Court makes landmark decision after illegal logging in NSW state forest
items.summary: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

items.title: Police shoot man during welfare check in Brisbane's north
items.summary: A 19-year-old man has been shot by police after officers were called to an Arana Hills address for a welfare check requested by his mother.

items.title: Conservation group plans to replant Daintree Rainforest trees on former sugarcane land
items.summary: A conservation group's plans to replant species from the World Heritage-listed Daintree Rainforest on land used for growing sugar have not won everybody over.

items.title: What to know about the four Artemis II astronauts heading to the Moon
items.summary: The Artemis II mission to the Moon could take four astronauts further from Earth than any human has gone before. Here's what we know about them.

items.title: March brought a rare blood moon, a rarer cyclone to WA — see WA's best weather photos
items.summary: Nature put on a special show for Western Australia in March, including a total lunar eclipse and rough seas from a powerful tropical cyclone, in addition to the spectacular sunsets the state is known for.

items.title: Australians reveal toll of fuel price rises on their lives as cost of living goes up
items.summary: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

items.title: Algal bloom tracks further north up the Yorke Peninsula, sparking concern for stingrays
items.summary: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

items.title: Australians expected to generate more solar panel waste each year by 2030, inquiry hears
items.summary: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

items.title: Zac Lomax to make Super Rugby debut for Western Force in Brisbane
items.summary: Zac Lomax will make his highly anticipated Super Rugby debut this weekend, almost a month to the day after his hopes of playing in the NRL this season were ended.

items.title: Fuel prices have begun to drop across capital cities as fuel excise cut kicks in today
items.summary: Consumers were warned the fuel excise cut might take weeks to be passed on to retailers, but prices have already begun to fall, NRMA says.

items.title: Police find $320,000 worth of allegedly stolen Lego, charge two people
items.summary: A man and woman will face court after allegedly stealing hundreds of Lego sets from retail outlets across metropolitan Adelaide.

items.title: Australia assessing how to protect Pacific nations from fuel shortages caused by Iran war
items.summary: Pacific nations are not yet suffering large-scale petrol shortages but are already starting to grapple with the economic impacts of the war, with fuel price hikes threatening to drive up inflation.

items.title: Mouse plague warning for Western Australia as researcher urges action
items.summary: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice, which are making their way into residential areas.

items.title: Fears for superb fairy-wrens as experts studying Canberra population predict extinction within decades
items.summary: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

items.title: GOAT is Australian artist Daniel Pozo's first project as a Sony lead
items.summary: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

items.title: Sam Konstas left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for 2026-27 season
items.summary: Sam Konstas is one of six players left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for the upcoming season.

items.title: Taylor Swift faces trademark lawsuit over The Life of a Showgirl album
items.summary: Las Vegas performer Maren Wade says the name and imagery of Taylor Swift's latest album are too close to those of her show, Confessions of a Showgirl.

items.title: Victoria Police formally identify Dezi Freeman as man shot dead in Thologolong
items.summary: Victoria Police have officially confirmed that the person shot dead by officers at a property in Victoria's north-east on Monday morning was fugitive Dezi Freeman.

items.title: An 'ebullient' feeling as markets price in an end to the Iran war, but there's a catch
items.summary: If you had woken up today and heard someone say Wall Street's tech sector rose more than 3 per cent overnight, you would expect it to be promptly followed by "April Fools' Day".

items.title: Anthony Albanese to urge Australians to 'play their part' in Iran response in TV address
items.summary: The prime minister's office says Anthony Albanese will address the nation on TV tonight, urging Australians to save fuel for areas and industries that "most need it".

items.title: Tottenham fan groups protest Roberto De Zerbi appointment over Mason Greenwood support
items.summary: Tottenham fan groups voice opposition to newly appointed manager Roberto De Zerbi over his previous public support for Mason Greenwood, a player who faced charges of attempted rape and assault.

items.title: Fuel crisis driving calls for better public transport in regional Western Australia
items.summary: Busselton has just three regular bus services, leaving 85-year-old Jan Whalan with an hour's trip on her mobility scooter to get to the shops — but as the fuel crisis also bites, there are calls for better public transport.

items.title: Seafood giant A Raptis and Sons to close after administrators fail to find buyer
items.summary: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

items.title: Seafood industry urges public to buy local for Easter as fuel costs bite
items.summary: Seafood operators are struggling to stay afloat, with the nation's biggest wild-caught tuna and swordfish company confirming it is shutting up shop.

items.title: NT government warned of 'significant' health risks in police watch houses
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Man accused of setting body on fire after alleged murder at Dimbulah in Far North Queensland
items.summary: A 37-year-old man has been arrested and charged with murder a week after burning human remains were found at a Dimbulah property.

items.title: Teenager paralysed in hay bale accident still determined to farm
items.summary: A Queensland teenager paralysed after a hay bale accident on his family farm is determined to return to the land despite the challenges of life in a wheelchair.

items.title: Italy fails to reach third straight World Cup after penalty shootout loss to Bosnia and Herzegovina
items.summary: Italy's World Cup exile continues after the four-time champions fall to a 4-1 penalty shootout defeat by Bosnia and Herzegovina.

items.title: NT tourism operators facing hundreds of thousands in damage costs after multiple devastating weather events
items.summary: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

items.title: Droving, selling or putting cattle on a boat, this is how NSW farmers are navigating the drought
items.summary: Farmers are turning to roadside grazing, selling up stock, and, in one case, shipping cattle to an island to navigate the drought and the rising cost of diesel.

items.title: Australia's Cooper Connolly makes stunning IPL debut with Punjab Kings
items.summary: Cooper Connolly puts in a game-winning performance for Punjab in his IPL debut.

items.title: Internal memos show NT government warned of 'significant risks' in watch houses
items.summary: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

items.title: From screen to symphony: Joff Bush on Bluey’s new orchestral album and music from the hit series
items.summary: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

items.title: Thermal imaging and night-time shooting key to Kangaroo Island corella control, authority says
items.summary: Thermal imaging, infrared technology and crack marksmanship are deployed to battle the island's growing little corella population.

items.title: Emerson Jones named in Australia's squad for Billie Jean King Cup tie against Great Britain
items.summary: At just 17, Emerson Jones is a chance to make her Billie Jean King Cup debut after being named in Australia's squad to face Great Britain.

items.title: Cocoa prices plummet but Easter chocolate stays expensive
items.summary: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

items.title: 'Giveaway' club loses licence after multiple breaches of law
items.summary: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

items.title: Bikie and money laundering claims taint Hellyer mine as government moves to shut it down
items.summary: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

items.title: Tiger Woods to seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI following car crash
items.summary: Tiger Woods says he will step away and seek treatment, days after he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

items.title: Government deadline for gun reform passes with less than half states and territories signed up
items.summary: The federal government's March deadline for national gun reform has come and gone, with more than half the states and territories still opposed to parts of the plan. Only four have committed to a gun buyback scheme.

items.title: Qld's hospitals tackle health of patients and planet in emission reduction efforts
items.summary: The WHO lists climate change as the single biggest health threat facing humanity, responsible for the deaths of 13 million people annually. Now, Queensland hospitals grapple with reducing their own environmental footprint.

items.title: Trump says Iran asked for a ceasefire, Tehran calls claim 'false' — as it happened
items.summary: The US president said America is "blasting Iran into oblivion" and claimed a senior figure from the regime has made contact.

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will all of Tony Popovic's experimentation pay off at the World Cup?
items.summary: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

items.title: Free dental clinic helps Adelaide residents access care in cost-of-living crisis
items.summary: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

items.title: Scientists discover more than 117 deep-sea creatures in Coral Sea
items.summary: More than 110 new fish and invertebrate species are discovered during a CSIRO voyage in the deep Coral Sea.

items.title: 80,000 Australian kids aged five and under not up to date with vaccinations
items.summary: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

items.title: Sydney developer who illegally cut down native trees lodges plan for $10m mansion
items.summary: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

items.title: Regional Australians disappointed by Vodafone ad claiming 'nothing's out here'
items.summary: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

items.title: Why your household could be hit by a bill hike trifecta this month
items.summary: Millions of Australian households will be hit with a trifecta of price hikes this month. Here's what to know about the changes and how they could affect you.

items.title: Dezi Freeman joins a long history of Australian fugitives captured or killed
items.summary: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

items.title: The government says it is 'situation normal' but are we already in a recession?
items.summary: The government is urging people to carry on as normal, despite fuel shortages, because it knows that panic will make matters worse. But it may need to consider more drastic economic intervention.

items.title: Experts say trigger for fuel rationing is not a 'precise' date
items.summary: The federal government is not expected to publicly nominate a clear trigger point for fuel rationing, despite internal modelling outlining potential thresholds.
